preamble: The transformation of the voiced bilabial nasal [m] to a voiced uvular nasal [ɴ]. This place shift occurs in specific positions, such as before a pause or following back vowels in certain languages.

examples.note: The Japanese mora nasal can derive from earlier bilabial nasals in coda positions, resolving to a uvular nasal word-finally.

sources: Vance, T. J. (2008). The Sounds of Japanese.
